pr.title: Match most of ftmetal, and rename related struct members
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Matched most of the previously empty ftmetal.c implementation and propagated metal-mode semantics through Fighter, common data, player/game-mode flags, draw/material code, walk/damage logic, and headers. The PR replaced several anonymous fields with meaningful metal-related names, added needed FighterBone/ftData/HSD_DObj typing, corrected symbol string boundaries for nearby data, and documented metal material usage. No substantive review discussion was present in the provided slice.
what_changed: Implemented roughly 250 lines in src/melee/ft/ftmetal.c, including ft_800C8170, ftCo_800C8348, ftCo_800C8438, ftCo_800C8540, ft_800C85B8, and static inline helpers for enabling metal state and DObj traversal.
Renamed Fighter metal-state fields: fp->x2223_b7 became fp->is_metal, fp->x2223_b6 became fp->is_always_metal, fp->x2028 became fp->metal_timer, and fp->x202C became fp->metal_health.
Renamed ftCommonData->unk_armor to ftCommonData->metal_armor and updated damage knockback armor logic to add metal armor only when fp->is_metal is set.

Updated fighter initialization, per-frame metal timer expiration, damage handling, copy/transfer logic, material selection, drawing, sound-effect selection, walk acceleration, and action cleanup paths to use the new metal field names.
Added struct typing needed by ftmetal matching: ftData gained HSD_Joint* x5C; FighterBone gained bitfields flags2_b0 through flags2_b7 plus xD; Fighter x2040 was typed as HSD_DObj**.
Updated ftmetal.h include guard to MELEE_FT_METAL_H, switched to <melee/ft/forward.h>, and named ftCo_800C8348 parameters as timer and health.
Added comments documenting ft_804D6580 as the metal material and game-mode/player match-info metal bits.
Adjusted config/GALE01/symbols.txt entries around lbl_803C6D24/lbl_803C6D30/lbl_803C6D54 and ftCo_804D3C90 to split/mark string data with corrected sizes.
Added a TODO near ft_800C80A4 noting it may share an inline with ftmetal functions.
Changed Player_SetFlagsBit5 call sites in gm_16AE.c from integer literals 1/0 to true/false, consistent with its apparent boolean meaning as the always-metal flag.
smart_moves: The PR did not just match ftmetal.c in isolation; it renamed all observed consumers of the same fields, making the semantic proof stronger across damage, rendering, audio, walking, player flags, and cleanup behavior.

Using is_metal versus is_always_metal captures an important distinction: temporary metal state can expire or be removed, while always-metal fighters such as classic Metal Mario should remain metal after ftCo_800C8540-style cleanup.

Naming metal_timer and metal_health reflects both expiration conditions observed in Fighter_8006A360 and damage decrement behavior in Fighter_TakeDamage_8006CC7C, reducing ambiguity around the old x2028/x202C fields.
The implementation preserves matching-sensitive idioms such as PAD_STACK calls, explicit NULL checks, ternary next-pointer traversal, and local inline helpers rather than over-cleaning control flow.
Splitting symbol table data into individual string labels avoids treating adjacent strings as one opaque object, which can be necessary for correct data references and matching.
Adding render/material-related bitfields to FighterBone made ftmetal.c able to reconstruct JObj flags from stored per-bone state instead of hard-coding unexplained behavior everywhere.
decomp_lessons: When a matched function reveals a struct member’s semantics, propagate the name across all users in the same PR so later matching work benefits immediately and stale xNNNN names do not obscure behavior.
Temporary status effects often need separate fields for active state, permanent/default state, timer, and remaining health; avoid collapsing these when naming fighter state.

Rendering-state restoration functions can expose latent struct bitfields: ft_800C8170 uses FighterBone flags2_b0 through flags2_b4 to rebuild JOBJ_LIGHTING, JOBJ_TEXGEN, JOBJ_SPECULAR, and related flags after metal mode.
For matching, keep compiler-shaped constructs such as PAD_STACK, redundant NULL checks, explicit local variables, and small static inline helpers if they are part of the codegen solution.
Symbol-table cleanup is part of decomp progress: correcting object sizes and marking data:string can unblock or stabilize matches involving nearby literals.
A boolean player/game-mode flag may become semantically identifiable only after tracing it into Fighter initialization; here Player_GetFlagsBit5 maps to Fighter::is_always_metal.
naming_conventions: Use positive boolean names for fighter bitfields when semantics are clear: is_metal and is_always_metal replaced x2223_b7 and x2223_b6.
Use domain-prefixed names for status-effect fields: metal_timer, metal_health, and metal_armor replaced anonymous x2028, x202C, and unk_armor.
Use parameter names in headers once semantics are known: ftCo_800C8348(Fighter_GObj*, int timer, int health).
Use file-scope include guards based on project path/name, as ftmetal.h changed from GALE01_0C8170 to MELEE_FT_METAL_H.
Document inferred fields with concise comments when useful, such as is_always_metal meaning classic Metal Mario and gm match-info x4_b0 meaning metal.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: ftmetal.c matching used PAD_STACK(8) and PAD_STACK(0x10) in multiple functions to satisfy stack layout/codegen constraints.

Static inline helpers such as enableMetal and dobj_next were introduced, likely to share exact code patterns and keep call sites/codegen manageable; a nearby TODO suggests ft_800C80A4 may use a shared inline with ftmetal functions.
The DObj traversal code preserves repeated NULL-safe ternary expressions like dobj = dobj != NULL ? dobj->next : NULL, which is a common matching tactic when simpler loops alter codegen.

ft_800C8170 reconstructs JObj flags from FighterBone bitfields, clears flags with 0x50180, sets computed flags, and switches on mobj->rendermode & RENDER_BLENDING to choose HSD_DObjModifyFlags parameters, with OSReport/HSD_Panic on unexpected blending flags.

ft_800C85B8 uses HSD_IDInsertToTable, ftAnim_GetNextJointInTree, HSD_DObjLoadDesc, HSD_DObjResolveRefsAll, HSD_JObjAddDObj/lb_8000CE30, hsdChangeClass(mobj, &ftMObj), and assert/report guards for DObj count limits.
Data-symbol matching included splitting lbl_803C6D24 into several string labels and correcting ftCo_804D3C90 from a 4-byte local object to a 1-byte local string-like object.
follow_up_queries: Search for remaining uses of x2223_b6, x2223_b7, x2028, x202C, or unk_armor to verify the metal naming propagation is complete.
Investigate the TODO in ft_0C31.c: determine whether ft_800C80A4 and ftmetal functions can share an inline without hurting matching.
Audit Fighter::x2040 typing: the diff shows HSD_DObj** but ft_800C85B8 stores DObj pointers into x2040[index], so confirm the intended declaration and downstream users.
Continue naming FighterBone flags2_b0 through flags2_b7 based on their observed JObj flag meanings and ftmetal loading behavior.
Look for remaining player flag bit names around Player_GetFlagsBit6/7 and Player_GetMoreFlagsBit1 that can be semantically identified from similar propagation.
